title: The Light to the Nations
subtitle: Mary carries the Child into the Temple, and old Simeon takes him into his arms

stillness: Be still, and let the temple grow quiet.
Simeon waited long years, sighing one desire:
to see, before he died, the One he longed for.
Wait now in that same silence.
gospel: When the fortieth day had come,
and the days of purification were fulfilled according to the Law of Moses,
Mary went out from the stable with Joseph and the Child,
though in her there was nothing to be purified,
for she had conceived without sin.
And they carried him up to Jerusalem,
to present him to the Lord in the temple,
and to give for him a sacrifice:
a pair of turtledoves, or two young pigeons.
behold: Attend to each thing, as if you were present.
Behold the Lord Jesus, already circumcised, carried up from Bethlehem.
Behold the Mother who needed no cleansing, bending herself to the Law.
She who was the Ark of the Covenant carries within her the Giver of the Law.
Behold her: gold within and gold without, shining with virtue inside and out.
She offers a candle to the Lord —
for Christ her Son is the lit candle:
flesh, and soul, and true Godhead.
This candle is offered for the human race,
and by it the night of our darkness is lit.
consider: He who was equal to the Father in all things did not think it enough to submit to a lowly Virgin —
he submitted himself to the Law.
The Master of humility humbled himself even in what he was not bound to,
that he might teach us humility.
Mary too, who had no cause and no need to be purified,
Hear Bernard: "Truly, blessed Virgin, you have no cause, you have no need of purifying.
But did your Son have need of circumcision?
Be among the women as one of them, for your Son too is in the midst of the children."
She could not teach in public by word, because of her sex —
so she willed to teach by example.
And this is what Simeon foretold to Mary, prophesying:
that a sword should pierce through her own soul.
The Son of Anna was rejected by the Jews;
the Son of Mary was condemned by them to a most shameful death.
Receive the light, but do not forget the sword.
prayer: who in the temple gave yourself mercifully to just Simeon,
who longed to see you, that he might hold you:
and to me who await you with all my desire, grant yourself kindly.
Whatever impurity you find in me, drive out by purifying grace;
and make my heart your temple, deigning to dwell there;
there let me embrace and hold you in the arms of desire.
Grant me always to desire you, the fountain of light, who are with the Father;
and let me not go out of this life
until I see you with the eyes of my heart —
you who are the love and the desire,
the life and the reward of those who desire you.

latinJewel.latin: Christus Mariæ Filius est candela accensa, propter tria quæ inveniuntur in ea, scilicet lumen, ignis, et cera; et in Christo sunt tria, scilicet caro, anima et deitas vera.
latinJewel.english: Christ, the Son of Mary, is the lighted candle, because of the three things found in it — light, fire, and wax; and in Christ there are three — flesh, soul, and true Godhead.
